UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user-specific information and preferences for the golf platform, excluding authentication credentials which are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: User's email address, primarily for identification purposes. Assumes authentication is handled by an external system.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, e.g., 'user' or 'admin'.

last5Scores.description: An array storing the user's last 5 Stableford golf scores. Scores must be between 1 and 45. New scores replace the oldest.

currentSubscriptionId.description: Reference to the user's currently active Subscription. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Subscription)

preferredCharityId.description: Reference to the Charity entity that the user prefers to donate to. (Relationship: Charity 1:N UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

SubscriptionPlan.title: SubscriptionPlan
SubscriptionPlan.description: Defines the different subscription plans available to users (e.g., monthly, yearly).

id.description: Unique identifier for the SubscriptionPlan entity.

name.description: The name of the subscription plan (e.g., 'Monthly Basic', 'Yearly Premium').

description.description: A detailed description of the subscription plan's features and benefits.

price.description: The monetary price of the subscription plan.

currency.description: The currency in which the subscription plan is priced (e.g., 'USD', 'GBP').

durationInMonths.description: The duration of the subscription plan in months (e.g., 1 for monthly, 12 for yearly).

isActive.description: Indicates whether the subscription plan is currently active and available for purchase.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription plan was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription plan was last updated.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Represents an individual user's active or past subscription to a SubscriptionPlan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this subscription. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Subscription)

planId.description: Reference to the SubscriptionPlan this subscription is based on. (Relationship: SubscriptionPlan 1:N Subscription)

startDate.description: The date when the subscription became active.

endDate.description: The date when the subscription is scheduled to expire or was cancelled.

status.description: The current status of the subscription (e.g., 'active', 'cancelled', 'expired', 'pending', 'trial').

paymentProviderSubscriptionId.description: An optional identifier from an external payment provider (e.g., Stripe) for this subscription.

charityContributionPercentage.description: The percentage of the subscription fee designated for charity by the user (minimum 10%).

totalAmountContributedToCharity.description: The cumulative amount contributed to charity from this specific subscription instance over its lifetime.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was last updated.

Charity.title: Charity
Charity.description: Stores information about charitable organizations that users can choose to support.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Charity entity.

name.description: The name of the charitable organization.

description.description: A detailed description of the charity's mission and activities, potentially AI-generated.

websiteUrl.description: The official website URL of the charity.

logoUrl.description: The URL to the charity's logo image.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the charity is currently active and available for user donations.

totalAmountReceived.description: The cumulative total amount of donations received by this charity through the platform.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the charity record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the charity record was last updated.

Draw.title: Draw
Draw.description: Represents a monthly prize draw event, including the winning numbers and associated details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Draw entity.

drawIdentifier.description: A human-readable identifier for the draw (e.g., '2023-10-MonthlyDraw').

drawMonth.description: The month in which the draw took place (1-12).

drawYear.description: The year in which the draw took place.

drawDate.description: The exact date and time when the draw was conducted.

winningNumbers.description: An array of the 5 random numbers generated for this draw (1-45).

status.description: The current status of the draw (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'verified', 'cancelled').

prizeDescription.description: An overall description of the prizes available for this draw, potentially AI-generated.

totalCharityContributionFromDraw.description: The total amount contributed to charity from all subscriptions active during this draw's period.

eligibleUserIds.description: References to UserProfile IDs who were eligible to participate in this draw. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Draw)

winnerWinningIds.description: References to Winning IDs awarded in this specific draw. (Relationship: Winning N:N Draw)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the draw record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the draw record was last updated.

Prize.title: Prize
Prize.description: Defines a template for a prize that can be won in a draw.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Prize entity.

name.description: The name of the prize (e.g., '5 Score Match Jackpot', '3 Score Match Prize').

description.description: A detailed description of the prize, potentially AI-generated.

value.description: The monetary value or estimated worth of the prize.

currency.description: The currency in which the prize value is expressed (e.g., 'USD').

matchCountRequired.description: The number of matching scores required to win this prize (e.g., 3, 4, 5).

imageUrl.description: The URL to an image representing the prize.

isActive.description: Indicates whether this prize is currently active and can be awarded.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the prize record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the prize record was last updated.

Winning.title: Winning
Winning.description: Records an instance of a user winning a specific prize in a particular draw.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Winning entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who won the prize. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Winning)

drawId.description: Reference to the Draw in which the prize was won. (Relationship: Draw 1:N Winning)

prizeId.description: Reference to the Prize entity that was awarded. (Relationship: Prize 1:N Winning)

matchCount.description: The number of scores the user matched to win this prize (e.g., 3, 4, 5).

winningScores.description: An array of the specific scores from the user's 'last5Scores' that matched the winning numbers in the draw.

claimStatus.description: The status of the prize claim (e.g., 'pending', 'claimed', 'verified', 'rejected', 'shipped').

claimDate.description: The date and time when the user claimed the prize, if applicable.

verificationDate.description: The date and time when an administrator verified the winning claim.

adminNotes.description: Optional notes added by an administrator regarding the winning or claim process.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the winning record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the winning record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. The `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid` and the `id` field within the document. This ensures private, user-owned data.
Defines available subscription plans. Documents are publicly readable but only editable by administrators.
Records a user's specific subscription. The `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid` and the `userId` field in the document, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the subscription.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific subscription record.

definition.description: Lists charitable organizations available for users to support. Documents are publicly readable but only editable by administrators.
Contains details about each monthly prize draw, including winning numbers and eligible users. Documents are generally publicly readable (e.g., after the draw date) and managed by administrators.
Defines templates for prizes that can be won in draws. Documents are publicly readable but only editable by administrators.

Records an individual user's prize winning. The `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid` and the `userId` field in the document, ensuring authorization independence. Also accessible by administrators for verification and management.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who won the prize.

params.name: winningId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific winning record.

definition.description: A dedicated top-level collection to store UIDs of administrative users. The existence of a document with a user's `uid` in this collection grants administrative privileges for Firestore security rules, adhering to the DBAC principle (Existence over Content).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and DBAC, ensuring a secure, scalable, and debuggable database. Authorization Independence is achieved primarily through denormalization: all user-specific subcollection documents (e.g., a user's Subscription or Winning record) explicitly include the `userId` field, which matches the `userId` in the document path. This allows Firestore Security Rules to verify `request.auth.uid == userId` and `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without performing expensive and non-atomic `get()` operations on parent documents. This makes rules clear, robust, and performant, enabling atomic writes.

For administrative roles, a dedicated top-level collection `/admin_users/{userId}` is used. This adheres to the 'Existence over Content' principle for DBAC (Database-Based Access Control). To grant admin privileges, a document exists in this collection with the admin's `uid` as its ID. Firestore rules can then efficiently check `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/admin_users/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()` for write access to global collections or sensitive user data, again avoiding complex hierarchical `get()` calls.

Structural Segregation is applied by grouping documents with similar security postures into distinct collections. Global entities like `SubscriptionPlans`, `Charities`, `Draws`, and `Prizes` reside in top-level collections, as they are generally publicly readable and only admin-editable. User-specific data (`UserProfile`, `Subscription`, `Winning`) is logically nested under `/users/{userId}/` paths, ensuring private data is isolated and easily secured based on ownership. This separation simplifies security rules by removing the need for complex conditional logic within a single collection.

QAPs (Queries as Policy) are supported inherently by this design. For user-specific data, the path-based authorization (`/users/{userId}/...`) ensures that `list` operations automatically filter by the `request.auth.uid`, making it impossible for a user to inadvertently query data belonging to another user. For global, publicly readable collections (`/subscriptionPlans`, `/charities`, `/draws`, `/prizes`), `list` operations are open to all, and client-side queries can apply additional filters (e.g., `isActive: true`). The security rules are designed to prevent unauthorized write access, but not to filter data for authorized reads, thereby upholding the QAP principle. The `Draw.eligibleUserIds` field, while containing sensitive UIDs, is part of a publicly readable document. If this list itself is considered sensitive until a certain point (e.g., after the draw is finalized), rules would need to specifically allow read only under certain conditions or for specific users; otherwise, it is assumed to be publicly consumable as part of the draw record for transparency.
